strategy: Attack with Myth, Life, or Death; Tamworth resists Balance 80% and takes +30% from those three. He is not stunable.
Do not kill the minions one at a time. Killing Lacombe (straw) escalates to Sandstorm every round, and then killing Berkshire (stick) escalates to Big Bad Hound every round.
Stack blades and clear all three houses together with one big AoE rather than picking them off in order.
Do not beguile the pigs. Their side-check reads a beguiled ally as gone and triggers the cheats early, sometimes double-cheating in one round.
Set up over a few rounds, then finish Lacombe, Berkshire, and Tamworth on the same turn with a single Myth, Life, or Death AoE.
cheatNotes: Once Lacombe (straw) is dead, Berkshire (stick) interrupt-casts Sandstorm every round. Do not kill the straw house early on its own.
Once Berkshire (stick) is dead, Tamworth (brick) interrupt-casts Big Bad Hound every round. Killing the minions in order just escalates the punishment; clear all three together with an AoE.
If Berkshire is beguiled and his turn comes before Tamworth, both the Sandstorm and Big Bad Hound cheats fire in one round.
If Lacombe is beguiled, only the Sandstorm cheat triggers.
Their cheats use a simple check for whether an ally is still present, so beguiling a pig reads as it being gone and triggers the cheat. Avoid beguiling them.
